body:
## Purpose 
- The purpose of this PR is to add the Ozark test to main, an experiment which demonstrates coupling the soil and the plant hydraulics models, and to update it so as to make it consistent with the changes brought to the plant hydraulics model this summer. This will link to issue #108. Co-authored with Katherine.

## To-do
- In a future PR, adjust the parameters, especially LAI, RAI, SAI and K_sat to better match site values in order to obtain a better agreement between water potential measurements at the Ozark site and model output. Explore changing the water retention curve to include a Psi50 parameter.

## Content
- The Ozark test was updated. The flux approximation function was simplified, the parameter space was reduced, the test is now in SI units and runs with the soil model, and diagnostics plots were included. All tasks listed in #108 are complete.

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevent documentation.
title: Ozark test
